recent_decisions.title: Approved Q3 2025/26 financial adjustments and capital programme
recent_decisions.political_summary: Cabinet approved portfolio adjustments, reserve transfers, and noted the revenue budget forecast and revised capital programme for Q3 2025/26. No vote counts recorded.

recent_decisions.title: Recommended Treasury Management policies for 2026/27
recent_decisions.political_summary: Cabinet recommended approval of Treasury Management policies, including the Capital Strategy, to Council. No vote counts recorded.

recent_decisions.title: Approved 2026/27 budget and Council Tax level
recent_decisions.political_summary: Cabinet approved the 2026/27 budget, Medium-Term Financial Plan, and Council Tax level. No vote counts recorded.

recent_decisions.title: Approved Authorities Monitoring Report 2024-2025
recent_decisions.political_summary: Cabinet approved the Draft Authorities Monitoring Report 2024-2025 for public inspection. No vote counts recorded.
key_data_points: Comments on infrastructure and deprivation data noted

recent_decisions.title: Noted Council Plan Monitoring Q3 2025/26
recent_decisions.political_summary: Cabinet noted the Q3 2025/26 performance report and endorsed remedial actions. No vote counts recorded.

recent_decisions.title: Hackney carriage licence limit retained at 160
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee voted to keep the limit of 160 hackney carriage licences, citing low unmet demand and public interest. No vote counts recorded.

recent_decisions.title: Draft Licensing Policy amended and consulted
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee approved amendments to the draft Licensing Policy and ordered a 6-week consultation. No vote counts recorded.
key_data_points: Major amendments may require committee review
Policy to be presented to Full Council for adoption

recent_decisions.committee: Overview and Scrutiny Climate Change Sub-Committee
recent_decisions.title: Council carbon footprint reduced by 51.2% vs 2018/19 baseline
recent_decisions.political_summary: The council's carbon footprint decreased by 51.2% compared to 2018/19, exceeding the target by 32.6% due to HVO, air source heat pumps, and solar PV. Heating remains the largest source of emissions (43.3%).
key_data_points: 51.2% reduction in carbon footprint vs 2018/19 baseline
Solar PV at Fleetwood YMCA saved 57% on costs

recent_decisions.committee: Overview and Scrutiny Climate Change Sub-Committee
recent_decisions.title: Sub-committee noted work programme update on EV charging points
recent_decisions.political_summary: The sub-committee noted the work programme update, with Councillor Fail suggesting data on charging point usage should inform future decisions on new installations.
key_data_points: Update on EV charging point usage data
Suggestion to use data for future charging point decisions

recent_decisions.title: Approval of previous meeting minutes
recent_decisions.political_summary: The minutes from the Extraordinary Council meeting on 20 November 2025 and the Council meeting on 27 November 2025 were approved by those present.

recent_decisions.title: Article 4 direction to restrict HMOs in Fleetwood, Thornton, and Cleveleys
recent_decisions.political_summary: Council intends to implement an Article 4 direction to require planning permission for HMO conversions in Fleetwood, Thornton, and Cleveleys.

recent_decisions.title: Retention of Armed Forces Covenant Silver Award
recent_decisions.political_summary: Council retained the Armed Forces Covenant Silver Award, recognizing its support for the armed forces community.

recent_decisions.title: Local Government Finance Settlement impact
recent_decisions.political_summary: Council noted a 2.5% reduction in funding from the Local Government Finance Settlement, but reassured members of strong financial management.

recent_decisions.title: Dog fouling and waste management measures
recent_decisions.political_summary: Council approved increased patrols, improved signage for dog fouling, and addressed waste collection calendar and alleyway cleanliness concerns.

recent_decisions.title: Refused listed building consent for door replacement at 1 Tanjon Court
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee refused listed building consent for a door replacement at 1 Tanjon Court, Fleetwood, citing harm to the historic character of the grade 2 listed building. The vote was unanimous.
key_data_points: Refused due to harm to historic character

motions.text: Proposal to refuse listed building consent for the replacement door and frame at 1 Tanjon Court, Fleetwood.

recent_decisions.title: Approved planning permission for equestrian development at Land North of Union Cottages
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee approved planning permission for the retention of stables and associated works at Land North of Union Cottages, Pilling, subject to conditions. The vote was unanimous.
key_data_points: Retrospective application for stables and fencing
No Biodiversity Net Gain assessment required

motions.text: Proposal to grant full planning permission for the proposed development subject to conditions and to authorise the Assistant Director of Planning and Building Control to make changes to the wording of the conditions.

recent_decisions.title: Approved revised Data Protection, Records Management, and Information Security Policies
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee approved updated policies on data protection, records management, and information security. No sensitive data is shared via WhatsApp, but its use is monitored.
key_data_points: Policies refreshed for clarity and readability
No sensitive data shared via WhatsApp

motions.text: Members resolved to approve the revised Data Protection Policy, Records Management Policy, and new Information Security Policy.

recent_decisions.title: Noted Internal Audit and Risk Management Progress Report
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted progress on internal audit and risk management, including cyber-security and counter-fraud work. Additional reports will be circulated later.
key_data_points: Audit plan completed with LCC support
Cyber-security report by Salford ICT Team

motions.text: Members noted the Internal Audit and Risk Management Progress Report and additional reports included at appendices 1-4.

recent_decisions.title: Noted Risk Management Risk Registers
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the risk registers and discussed strategic and operational risks, including contaminated land. Wording may be amended to reflect public health aspects.
key_data_points: Risk registers discussed at each meeting
Contaminated land risk wording may be amended
LCC role as a control measure noted

motions.text: Members noted the Risk Management Risk Registers and discussed strategic and operational risks.

recent_decisions.title: Noted Annual Review of Audit and Standards Committee Performance
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee reviewed its performance against CIPFA’s Self-Assessment of Good Practice. Nine members completed the questionnaire.
key_data_points: Supports signing off Annual Governance Statement

motions.text: Members noted the Annual Review of the Audit and Standards Committee’s Performance.

recent_decisions.title: ICB reports £5.5bn healthcare funding and 47% cost reduction target
recent_decisions.political_summary: The NHS Integrated Care Board manages £5.5bn in healthcare funding and must cut running costs by 47% without reducing service investment. Key priorities include winter health campaigns and mental health support for young people.
recent_decisions.financial_context: Total healthcare funding managed by the ICB
key_data_points: £5.5bn in healthcare funding managed by ICB
Focus on winter health campaigns and mental health support

recent_decisions.title: Wyre Borough Economic Development and Visitor Economy Strategy discussed
recent_decisions.political_summary: The council discussed the Economic Development and Visitor Economy Strategy, with members questioning its adaptability to business rate increases affecting hospitality businesses. The strategy offers up to 15 years of support for hospitality businesses.
key_data_points: Strategy provides up to 15 years of support for hospitality businesses
Flexible frameworks for quicker, targeted support if needed
Business rate increases impacting hospitality businesses

recent_decisions.title: Approved temporary event notice for Joe's Bar with police conditions
recent_decisions.political_summary: The Licensing Committee approved a temporary event notice for Joe's Bar to extend trading hours to 3am on 27 December 2025, subject to police conditions. Lancashire Police objected but agreed to conditions, and no other objections were raised.
key_data_points: Police conditions agreed by premises user

motions.text: The Licensing Committee decided to approve the temporary event notice for Joe's Bar with the conditions proposed by Lancashire Police.

recent_decisions.title: Licensing policy revised and consultation approved
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee agreed to amend the draft Licensing Policy and conduct a 6-week consultation. Major amendments may require further approval.
key_data_points: 6-week consultation period for trade and stakeholders
Policy may return to committee if major amendments made
Final policy to be presented to Full Council for adoption

recent_decisions.title: Retirement Policy updated to reflect government pension age changes
recent_decisions.political_summary: The council updated its Retirement Policy to reflect the government's increase in the minimum pension age from 55 to 57 from April 2028. The two-year vesting period was also referenced.
key_data_points: Pension age increased to 57 from April 2028

motions.text: The committee approved the updates to the Employee Code of Conduct, Recruitment and Selection Policy, Safeguarding Policy, Employee Training and Development Policy, and Zero Tolerance Policy.

recent_decisions.title: Flexible Retirement Policy amended to reduce maximum period
recent_decisions.political_summary: The council reduced the maximum period for flexible retirement from three years to two years and clarified that requests are not guaranteed but considered against operational feasibility.
key_data_points: Maximum flexible retirement period reduced to two years
No absolute right to flexible retirement

recent_decisions.title: Leave and Work-life Balance Policy updated with minor changes
recent_decisions.political_summary: The council updated its Leave and Work-life Balance Policy to require staff to confirm leave approval before booking holidays and extended additional leave to Cadet Force Adult Volunteers.
key_data_points: Staff must confirm leave approval before booking holidays
Additional leave extended to Cadet Force Adult Volunteers
Sick staff cannot perform election duties

recent_decisions.title: Extended Authorised Absence Policy amended
recent_decisions.political_summary: The council made minor amendments to the Extended Authorised Absence Policy.
key_data_points: Minor amendments made throughout the policy

recent_decisions.title: Domestic Abuse Policy updated with minor changes
recent_decisions.political_summary: The council made minor amendments to the Domestic Abuse Policy.
key_data_points: Minor amendments made throughout the policy

recent_decisions.title: Equal Opportunities Policy amended
recent_decisions.political_summary: The council made minor amendments to the Equal Opportunities Policy.
key_data_points: Minor amendments made throughout the policy

recent_decisions.title: Preventing Illegal Working Policy updated following government guidance
recent_decisions.political_summary: The council made minor amendments to the Preventing Illegal Working Policy following government guidance.
key_data_points: Minor amendments made following government guidance

recent_decisions.title: Approval of three unitary model proposal for Lancashire
recent_decisions.political_summary: Cabinet approved submitting a three unitary model proposal for Lancashire to the government by 28 November 2025. The Leader of the Council was delegated to finalise the submission.
key_data_points: Three unitary model proposed for Lancashire
Leader of the Council delegated to finalise submission

motions.text: That Cabinet approve the attached submission of a proposal for a three unitary model for Lancashire be submitted to the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government by the deadline of the 28 November 2025.
That Cabinet delegates finalising the full submission document to the Leader of the Council, in consultation with the Chief Executive, prior to submitting to the Government by 28 November 2025.

recent_decisions.title: Noting Medium-Term Financial Plan mid-year update
recent_decisions.political_summary: Cabinet noted the updated Medium-Term Financial Plan forecast without further action.
key_data_points: Medium-Term Financial Plan mid-year update presented
No financial adjustments or new commitments made

motions.text: To note the attached Medium-Term Financial Plan (MTFP) mid-year forecast update.

recent_decisions.title: Endorsement of Council Plan Monitoring Quarter Two 2025/26
recent_decisions.political_summary: Cabinet noted the quarterly performance report and endorsed remedial actions to address project delays.
key_data_points: Quarterly performance report for Q2 2025/26 reviewed
Remedial actions endorsed to address project delays

motions.text: Cabinet is asked to note the report and that the remedial actions to get projects and measures back on course are endorsed.

recent_decisions.title: Exclusion of public and press for Leisure Management Contract Award
recent_decisions.political_summary: Cabinet agreed to exclude the public and press during discussion of the Leisure Management Contract Award due to exempt financial information.
key_data_points: Public and press excluded under Local Government Act 1972
Exempt information related to financial or business affairs

motions.text: Cabinet agreed that the public and press be excluded from the meeting whilst agenda item 9 was being considered, as it referred to exempt information as defined in category 3 (Information relating to the financial or business affairs of any particular person (including the authority holding that information)) of Part 1 of Schedule 12(a) of the Local Government Act, 1972, as amended by the Local Government (Access to Information) Variation Order 2006 and the public interest in maintaining the exe

recent_decisions.title: Approval of Leisure Management Contract with Parkwood Leisure
recent_decisions.political_summary: Cabinet approved a 15-year contract with Parkwood Leisure for leisure management, with a £2 million investment fund. The contract includes TUPE transfers for staff.
key_data_points: Option to extend contract by 5 years

motions.text: That approval is given to award the Leisure Contract to the Preferred Leisure Provider, Parkwood Leisure as set out in Appendix A and B, (both exempt) for an initial 15-year contract period with the option to extend by a further five years.
